channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Study finds lunar polar ice deposits are stable and ancient based on Chandrayaan-2 data
item.description: An international team of scientists, including researchers from India’s Physical Research Laboratory (PRL), IISER, and Institute of Remote Sensing, has discovered that water ice trapped in the permane...

item.title: Meitei groups urge authorities to respond following deadly attack on BSF soldier's home
item.description: In a tragic incident that has shocked local communities, Meitei groups have called for urgent governmental action after an attack on a Border Security Force (BSF) soldier's residence. The assault resu...

item.title: Government to launch PUV service contracting program with payments per kilometer and fare discounts
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. announced that starting April 15, 2026, the Department of Transportation will roll out a service contracting program to support public utility vehicle (PUV) d...

item.title: B.C. High School Students Launch Fundraiser to Save Music Programs Amid Budget Cuts
item.description: In the face of proposed budget cuts that threaten their middle school music programs, three high school students from Victoria, B.C., have launched a fundraising campaign to raise $250,000. The propos...

item.title: Queensland MP Jimmy Sullivan found dead in Brisbane
item.description: Independent Queensland MP Jimmy Sullivan has been found dead in Brisbane. Authorities have not yet released further details about the circumstances surrounding his death, but the discovery has shocked...

item.title: Bulelwa Jordan-Tati on the emotional depth and purpose of South African interior design
item.description: Bulelwa Jordan-Tati, founder of Urban Blend Interiors in Cape Town, has earned recognition for her ability to create interiors that combine functionality, beauty and emotional resonance. With a backgr...

item.title: Boko Haram Attack in Borno Kills Army General, Many Soldiers and Brigade Imam
item.description: Boko Haram launched a deadly attack on a military base in Borno state, targeting the Joint Task Force in Benesheikh, Kaga Local Government Area, on Wednesday night. Insurgents from the IS-backed facti...

item.title: Los Angeles plans $360 million in affordable housing funded by mansion tax
item.description: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass, along with City Councilmember Ysabel Jurado, has proposed allocating over $360 million to developers and nonprofits to build and preserve affordable housing across the ci...

item.title: Netanyahu warns Israel ready to continue military action despite US-Iran ceasefire
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu don make am clear say war no don finish yet, even as United States and Iran don agree small ceasefire. For public address, Netanyahu talk say Israel go still ...

item.title: Tanzania faces high fuel prices amid international supply disruptions and policy responses
item.description: Fuel prices have become a major focus of discussion across Tanzania as global oil markets continue to experience instability. Although international crude prices eased slightly following a temporary t...

item.title: Learn the Meaning of the Ilocano Word 'Kabsat' for April 10, 2026
item.description: ABS-CBN Lifestyle presents the Word of the Day for April 10, 2026: 'kabsat.' This word originates from the Ilocano language, widely spoken in Northern Philippines. 'Kabsat' is often used to refer to a...

item.title: UK Defence Secretary reports Russian submarines targeted national communication cables
item.description: The UK Defence Secretary has revealed that three Russian submarines were identified as targeting critical undersea communication cables connecting the United Kingdom with the rest of the world. These ...

item.title: Nitish Kumar to be sworn in as Rajya Sabha member
item.description: The news reports that Nitish Kumar, a prominent Indian politician and leader of the Janata Dal (United), is scheduled to take oath as a member of the Rajya Sabha today. The Rajya Sabha is the upper ho...

item.title: Middle East ceasefire talks underway as tensions rise over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Tensions in the Middle East have escalated as Israeli airstrikes on Lebanon and Iran’s control over the Strait of Hormuz continue to dominate headlines. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has a...

item.title: Why Michael Oliver Cannot Officiate Matches Involving Newcastle United and Sunderland
item.description: The Premier League has officially appointed Michael Oliver to take charge of Arsenal’s upcoming match against Bournemouth at the Emirates Stadium. The 41-year-old referee, who is among England’s most ...

item.title: Over 250 Poodle-Cross Dogs Rescued from Overcrowded Home and Doing Well, RSPCA Reports
item.description: Over 250 poodle-cross dogs were rescued in January from an overcrowded home in the UK, where they were found crammed into a living room in appalling conditions. Despite their initial state, the dogs a...

item.title: Congress Withdraws Candidate from Baramati By-election Following Pawar Family Request
item.description: In a late development just an hour before the deadline for withdrawal of nominations, the Congress party decided to pull its candidate from the Baramati by-election. The decision came following persis...

item.title: Woman Charged Following Fatal Fire Attack on Partner
item.description: Gauteng police are investigating a shocking case where a 25-year-old woman allegedly poured petrol on her partner, the father of her child, and left him to burn in bed. The victim, 25-year-old Misokuh...

item.title: Closure of Strait of Hormuz amid Middle East tensions pushes oil prices higher
item.description: The CEO of Abu Dhabi National Oil Company, Sultan Al Jaber, has confirmed that the Strait of Hormuz remains closed, despite a US-Iran ceasefire announced earlier this week. Passage through the strateg...

item.title: Updates on 24 Sussex Drive renovations and political shifts involving Marilyn Gladu
item.description: As of April 9, 2026, several notable developments are being tracked in Canada. The prime minister’s official residence, 24 Sussex Drive, remains unoccupied and in a state of serious disrepair over a d...
